questions.question: What do the `track()` and `trigger()` callbacks in `customRef` do?
options: `track()` logs the read to Vue DevTools; `trigger()` forces a full component re-mount
`track()` tells Vue this ref was read so dependents should re-run on change; `trigger()` notifies dependents that the value changed
`track()` starts recording mutations; `trigger()` replays them in order
`track()` subscribes a watcher; `trigger()` manually fires the watcher callback

questions.question: In a debounced `customRef`, when is `trigger()` actually called?
options: On every keystroke to keep the template in sync with the raw input value
Only after the user stops typing for the specified delay period
Once on `mounted` and again on each `updated` lifecycle hook
Immediately on `set()`, with the DOM update deferred to the next tick

questions.question: What rule must NEVER be broken when implementing `customRef`?
options: Do not call `track()` more than once per `get()` invocation
Do not call `trigger()` inside `get()` as it creates an infinite loop
Do not return a value from `set()` because it is ignored
Do not use `customRef` inside a composable that is called in `<script setup>`
